title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Required Check Stable Identity
summary: A pipeline runs formatter, lint, type, tests, and build under one continue-on-error step, reports green after skipped work, and protects the branch with a mutable job name. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci required check stable identity increment in quality gate dependency graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design stable job names, required status checks, merge queues, branch rules, bypass authority, and migration without accidental deadlock.
Distinguish success, failure, cancelled, skipped, neutral, timed out, and continued failures in job and required-check policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Design stable job names, required status checks, merge queues, branch rules, bypass authority, and migration without accidental deadlock. A defensible solution applies Ci Required Check Stable Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to quality gate dependency graph.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-required-check-stable-identity — Design stable job names, required status checks, merge queues, branch rules, bypass authority, and migration without accidental deadlock.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: worked ci quality gate orchestration 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Renaming a required job automatically updates every repository rule. This fails the Ci Required Check Stable Identity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml

    case-key: 1e0jds
